Name: robust_mixed_precision_quantization
Title: Robust Mixed-Precision Quantization: Exploring Layer Sensitivity and Perturbation Resilience
Short Hypothesis: Training-free mixed-precision quantization methods can be significantly improved by incorporating a structured approach to assess layer sensitivity and perturbation resilience, leading to more stable and accurate models across diverse conditions.
Related Work: Existing works such as SliM-LLM and EMQ focus on adaptive mixed-precision techniques but do not comprehensively evaluate the impact of layer interdependencies and robustness against external perturbations. This proposal aims to fill that gap by introducing a systematic approach to quantization that considers these factors.
Abstract: This research proposes a novel framework for robust mixed-precision quantization (MPQ) that emphasizes the evaluation of layer sensitivity and the resilience of models against perturbations. While recent advancements in training-free MPQ have demonstrated efficiency gains, they often overlook the intricate dependencies between layers and their vulnerability to input variations. Our approach involves developing a layer-wise sensitivity analysis based on mutual information metrics, enabling adaptive bit-width assignments that enhance robustness. We will conduct extensive empirical evaluations to benchmark the proposed framework against standard MPQ techniques in scenarios involving structured perturbations. Our experiments aim to demonstrate improved accuracy and stability, contributing to the deployment of robust models in real-world applications. The findings will provide insights into the inter-layer dynamics of MPQ and offer practical guidelines for implementation in latency-sensitive contexts.
Experiments: 1. Evaluate the proposed robustness framework against standard MPQ methods under structured perturbations, such as noise and adversarial attacks, using metrics like accuracy, stability, and computational efficiency.
2. Conduct a layer sensitivity analysis using mutual information to assess the impact of layer dependencies on quantization performance, comparing with existing techniques.
3. Implement a training-free bit-width allocation strategy based on sensitivity scores and evaluate its effectiveness in maintaining model accuracy across various tasks.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed approach may require extensive experimental validation across multiple architectures, which could be time-consuming.
Layer sensitivity metrics might not capture all aspects of model behavior under perturbations, potentially limiting the robustness assessment.
The effectiveness of the approach may vary across different datasets and applications, necessitating further adaptations.
